scenario: Northwind Retailer uses Camunda 8.8 to run an ad-hoc sub-process that exposes 12 inner elements to an AI-driven job worker for an order-processing flow handling 800 orders per day. A developer needs to read the metadata Zeebe publishes for each activatable inner element so the worker can decide which elements to activate next.
question: Which list of per-element fields is exposed on each entry of the adHocSubProcessElements variable in the ad-hoc sub-process scope?

options.text: elementId, elementName, documentation, properties, and parameters defined via the fromAi FEEL function on each activatable inner element entry
elementId, elementType, jobType, retries, and headers configured on the inner element are exposed on each activatable inner element entry
id, name, description, tags, and fromAi parameters from the BPMN extension elements are exposed on each activatable inner element entry
elementId, elementName, executionListeners, inputMappings, and outputMappings are exposed on each activatable inner element entry

a.text: Correct. The documentation lists exactly elementId, elementName, documentation, properties, and parameters (populated via the fromAi FEEL function) as the per-element fields published on each entry of the adHocSubProcessElements variable.

b.text: Incorrect. Fields such as elementType, jobType, retries, and headers are not part of the documented adHocSubProcessElements entry schema; the variable exposes activation metadata, not job configuration internals.

c.text: Incorrect. The documented field names are elementId and elementName, not id and name, and description and tags are not part of the per-element metadata published on adHocSubProcessElements.

d.text: Incorrect. executionListeners, inputMappings, and outputMappings are not exposed via adHocSubProcessElements; the documented per-element fields are elementId, elementName, documentation, properties, and parameters.

explanation: Each entry in the adHocSubProcessElements variable exposes elementId, elementName, documentation, properties, and parameters, with parameters populated from fromAi FEEL function calls on the inner element; the variable is read-only metadata for job workers and should not be updated.
